name: Sailcloth Tent
text: Tier-0. A sail slung over two poles. It won't turn a storm, but it's yours, and the flats feel smaller with a roof of your own.
purpose: A cheap shelter you can pitch anywhere — no village needed. Fall on the flats and you wake here, not out in the cold.

name: Driftwood Wall
text: Tier-0. Keeps out the wind and the optimists.
purpose: A wall segment. Blocks hounds and marks your ground.

name: Driftwood Cot-Hut
text: Tier-0. Four walls of wave-worn wood and a cloth roof. The dark stays outside; that's the whole trick of houses.
purpose: Sleeps two villagers. At night they go inside — rested, and safe from what walks the flats.

name: Wreck-Timber Workbench
text: Tier-0. Every village starts as a plank at working height.
purpose: Crafting station. Unlocks salt, tools, and votives when you stand near it.

name: Yoke-Post
text: Holds the Taken while you decide who you are. Not grim by itself — what happens next is.
purpose: Holds a captured raider until you break or free them. (capture: coming soon)

name: Chapel
text: Dedicated to one god at consecration. Rank-2 attunement needs one; a priest will come.
purpose: Your god's house. Hold rites here [E] to restore their Vigor; enshrine remnants.

name: Great Hearth
text: The center of every Halor village. Feeds him a little every evening it's warm.
purpose: The village heart — settlers gather here. Tended, it feeds Halor favor.

name: Smokehouse
text: Meat curing inside is a small prayer to the Salt-Father.
purpose: Cures raw meat into smoked food and salt rations. Tended, it feeds Halor.

name: Salt Cellar
text: What is kept well, keeps.
purpose: Food storage that slows spoilage. Tended, it feeds Halor.

name: Lightning-Rod
text: Real protection in the great storms — and every bolt it takes is a hymn.
purpose: Storm protection in a radius during the great storms. Feeds Maren favor.

name: Storm-Cistern
text: Catches what the Storm-Mother throws. She likes being kept.
purpose: Collects rain during storms. Feeds Maren favor.

name: Tide-Bell
text: Rings the hours of a tide that isn't there. Neris keeps count anyway.
purpose: Marks Neris's tide-rhythm hours. Feeds her favor. (effects: coming soon)

name: Healing Bath
text: Warm brine, drawn right. The closest thing to the sea's embrace left standing.
purpose: Slowly heals whoever rests in it. Feeds Neris. (effects: coming soon)

name: Way-Beacon
text: A flag on the flats. Vessa counts every traveler it saves an hour.
purpose: A travel marker. Feeds Vessa favor. (effects: coming soon)

name: Beast Pen
text: Old Ghal's first commandment: keep what keeps you.
purpose: Keeps tamed beasts. Feeds Old Ghal. (taming: coming soon)

name: The Salt-Wheel

grimRules.kinderAlternative: The Unbinding: good treatment + time converts the Taken into true villagers with no instrument at all.
grimRules.recipeSource: A slaver's wreck in the Shallows; the manifest is worse than the wheel.
grimRules.testimony: Priests won't walk past it without muttering. Pilgrims arrive slower. Saved Peoples refuse to trade in sight of it. Ur-Noth's voice warms with every turn.

text: Breaks the Taken quickly into the Broken. Feeds the Unlit with every turn. You will never need it. That's the point.
purpose: GRIM: breaks a captive fast into the Broken. Feeds Ur-Noth. (coming soon)

name: Rendering-Vat

grimRules.kinderAlternative: Remnants can be enshrined (strengthens a god) or traded whole; consuming raw works without the vat, just at worse yield.
grimRules.recipeSource: Whispered, not found. He offers it the third time you hold a remnant too long.
grimRules.testimony: It hums at night. The villagers have all, independently, decided never to mention it.

text: Consumes god-remnants at better yield. The gods know exactly what it is.
purpose: GRIM: consumes god-remnants at better yield. Feeds Ur-Noth. (coming soon)

name: Drill-Yard
text: A sparring post and a ring of scuffed sand. Of course the Storm-Mother owns the place where villagers learn to hit back.
purpose: Villagers train an Arms class here — assign one and the drill days add up. Tended, it feeds Maren.

name: Memorial Stone
text: Salt-scoured stone, one name at a time. The homestead god keeps the dead, and a village that keeps its dead grieves half as long.
purpose: The village keeps its dead — names go here, and grief days halve. Tended, it feeds Halor.

name: Altar of the Salt-Father
text: A shelf of salt-scoured stone at the chapel's heart, cut for things worth keeping. Halor has never asked for gold. He asks what you're willing to not eat.
purpose: Halor's altar. Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen his rites.

name: Altar of the Squall
text: Bronze fittings bolted to a slab that's taken a strike before and kept standing. Maren doesn't want quiet devotion. She wants to see what you brought her.
purpose: Maren's altar. Relics displayed and offerings laid here strengthen her rites.
